_meta.description: Market archetypes for startup financing terms, keyed by market regime. Used to fill defaults when an employee does not know the exact cap-table terms. Every figure is a market median or a documented risk-scenario value, NOT a guarantee about any specific company. Provenance: see data/sources.md.
_meta.dilution_note: dilution_per_round = median primary dilution sold to investors in that round (employee shares are diluted alongside). Carta medians, 2024-2025.
_meta.investor_ownership_note: investor_ownership_pct = cumulative as-converted ownership held by preferred investors by that stage; drives conversion/participation math in Simple mode. Approximate, from typical post-Series-A/B/C cap tables.

2021_hot_market.label: 2021 — Hot market (founder-friendly terms)
2021_hot_market.note: Peak-market financings: clean 1x non-participating preferred was near-universal; the value-destruction risk comes mainly from over-raising and dilution, not from structure.

2025_market_standard.label: 2025 — Market standard (clean terms, current medians)
2025_market_standard.note: The base case for a healthy priced round today: 1x non-participating preferred. Carta medians show per-round dilution drifting DOWN over 2023-2025 (companies selling smaller slices).

structured_downround.label: Structured / down round (risk scenario, NOT the median)
structured_downround.note: Illustrative downside, modeled on the rise of investor-protective 'structured' terms in 2022-2024 down markets: senior and >1x preferences, participation with caps, pay-to-play. This is the tail the tool exists to expose, not a prediction for your company.

_exit_outcomes._calibration: Exit-value distribution presets (multiple of the last post-money valuation V0). The 'Series A' preset is FIT to Correlation Ventures: ~65% of outcomes below 1x and ~4% above 10x. Other stages are directional adjustments for the documented decline in failure rate at later stages (Series A->B ~35% fail; Series B+ failure drops sharply). Each preset is a lognormal-survivor + failure-mass mixture; see tov/montecarlo.py.
